faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How do I convert PNG to JPG online?
faq.a1: Simply upload your PNG file, select JPG as the output format, then click Process. Your converted file will be ready to download instantly — no server upload required.
faq.q2: What is the difference between WebP and JPG?
faq.a2: WebP is a newer image format that produces much smaller files than JPG while looking just as good. It also supports transparent backgrounds like PNG. Most modern browsers and apps support it — it's the recommended format for websites today.
faq.q3: Does converting image formats affect quality?
faq.a3: It depends on the formats. Converting from JPG to PNG won't lose quality. But converting between JPG and WebP/AVIF may cause a very slight quality reduction — in most cases you won't notice the difference. PixelSwift uses the best possible settings to keep your images looking great.
faq.q4: What is the best image format for websites?
faq.a4: For websites, WebP or AVIF are the best choices — smaller file sizes, great quality, and both support transparent backgrounds. If you need to support very old browsers, use JPG. If you need a perfectly sharp image with transparency, use PNG.
faq.q5: Can I convert multiple images at once?
faq.a5: Yes! PixelSwift supports batch conversion of up to 100 images at once. Simply drag and drop all your files, select the output format, and they'll be processed simultaneously.
faq.q6: How do I convert images while keeping transparency?
faq.a6: To keep transparency, convert to PNG, WebP, or AVIF format — all three support transparent backgrounds. JPG does not support transparency and will replace transparent areas with a white background.

guide.case4Desc: Convert web images to high-quality JPG or PNG for printing. Ensure your flyers, posters, and business cards look sharp and professional.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How to compress images without losing quality?
faq.a1: Set the quality slider to 80-90%. At this level, the image looks virtually the same but the file size drops significantly. Use the side-by-side comparison view to check the result before downloading.
faq.q2: What is the difference between compressing PNG and JPG?
faq.a2: JPG compression makes the file smaller by reducing image detail — the lower the quality setting, the smaller the file. PNG compression works differently — it simplifies colors to shrink the file while keeping the image looking nearly identical. PixelSwift handles both automatically.
faq.q3: How much can I reduce an image file size?
faq.a3: Typically, JPG images can be reduced by 40-70%, and PNG images by 20-50%. The exact result depends on the image itself — photos with many colors compress less than simple graphics. Want even smaller files? Try converting to WebP or AVIF format with our Converter tool.
faq.q4: What is the ideal image size for a website?
faq.a4: For websites, each image should ideally be under 200KB. Large banner images can be 300-500KB. Set the quality slider to 75-85% for the best balance between image quality and loading speed. Smaller images = faster website = better Google ranking.
faq.q5: Can I compress images for email attachments?
faq.a5: Yes! Most email providers limit attachments to 25MB. Use PixelSwift to compress your images to a smaller size — set quality to 70-80% to reduce photos significantly while keeping them looking great in emails.
faq.q6: Is PixelSwift better than TinyPNG for compression?
faq.a6: Both produce excellent results. The key difference: TinyPNG's free tier limits you to 500 images per month, while PixelSwift has zero limits — unlimited compression, free, no sign-up required, and you can batch process up to 100 images at once.

guide.step1Desc: Drag and drop files or click to browse. Supports JPG, PNG, WebP, and AVIF — compress up to 100 images at once.

guide.case1Desc: Reduce image sizes by up to 90% to make pages load faster. Faster pages rank higher on Google and keep visitors engaged longer.
guide.case2Desc: Compress photos to fit email size limits (25MB for Gmail, Outlook). Send high-quality images without bounced emails.
guide.case3Desc: Bulk compress your phone gallery to free up gigabytes of storage. Keep all your photos while using a fraction of the space.
guide.case4Desc: Compress product images for Shopify, Amazon, or Etsy. Faster-loading product pages mean more sales and happier customers.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How do I resize an image to a specific size?
faq.a1: Upload your image, enter the exact width and height in pixels, or use percentage mode to scale proportionally. You can also use quick presets for common sizes like 1:1, 4:3, or 16:9. Click Resize & Download to get your resized image.
faq.q2: Does resizing an image reduce quality?
faq.a2: Making an image smaller may reduce quality slightly, but PixelSwift uses smart processing to keep the result as sharp as possible. Making an image larger can cause some blurriness — it's always best to start with a high-resolution original.
faq.q3: What is the difference between scaling and cropping?
faq.a3: Scaling changes the overall dimensions of your entire image — making it larger or smaller while keeping all content visible. Cropping cuts away parts of the image to focus on a specific area. PixelSwift's resizer uses scaling to preserve your complete image.
faq.q4: What are the best image sizes for social media?
faq.a4: Common sizes: Instagram post 1080×1080px, Instagram story 1080×1920px, Facebook cover 820×312px, Twitter/X header 1500×500px, LinkedIn banner 1584×396px. Use PixelSwift's quick presets to resize to these dimensions easily.
faq.q5: Can I resize images without stretching or distortion?
faq.a5: Yes. Enable 'Lock Aspect Ratio' (on by default) — when you change the width, the height adjusts automatically to maintain the original proportions. This prevents any stretching or squishing.
faq.q6: What image resolution (DPI) should I use for printing?
faq.a6: For printing photos, your image needs to be large enough — about 300 pixels per inch of print. For example, to print a standard 4×6 inch photo, you need at least a 1200×1800 pixel image. For screens and websites, smaller sizes work fine.

guide.stepsTitle: How to Resize Images in 3 Steps
guide.step1Desc: Drag and drop your file or click to select one. Supports JPG, PNG, WebP, and AVIF up to 50MB.
guide.step2Desc: Enter exact pixel values, use percentage scaling, or pick a preset like 1:1 Square or 16:9 Widescreen.
guide.step3Desc: Click Download and your resized image is saved instantly. Rotate or flip it before downloading if needed.
guide.case1Desc: Resize to 1080×1080 for feed posts, 1080×1920 for Stories and Reels. Get pixel-perfect uploads every time.
guide.case2Desc: Resize hero images and banners to the exact dimensions your site requires. Avoid stretched or blurry images.
guide.case3Desc: Resize photos to exact passport or visa requirements. Set precise pixel dimensions for official documents.
guide.case4Desc: Resize product photos to marketplace standards — Amazon (2000×2000), eBay (1600×1600), Shopify. Consistent sizing looks professional.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.overviewText: PixelSwift is an online image processing tool that runs entirely in your browser. We take your privacy seriously, and this policy explains how we handle your data.
privacy.dataText: PixelSwift does not collect, store, or transmit any of your image files or personal data related to your images. Specifically:
privacy.dataItem1: Your images are never uploaded to any server
privacy.dataItem2: All image processing is performed locally in your browser
privacy.dataItem3: We do not collect any personally identifiable information through our image tools
privacy.localText: All image conversion, compression, and resizing features of PixelSwift are executed entirely within your browser. Your files never leave your device, meaning you have complete control over your data.
privacy.cookiesText: PixelSwift uses essential functional cookies to save your language preference and theme settings. Google Analytics also sets first-party cookies to collect anonymous usage data (see Analytics section below).
privacy.analyticsText: We use Google Analytics 4 (GA4) to understand how visitors use our website. GA4 is loaded directly without requiring cookie consent. It collects anonymous, aggregated data including:
privacy.analyticsItem1: Pages visited and time spent on each page
privacy.analyticsItem2: Browser type, device type, and operating system
privacy.analyticsItem3: General geographic location (country/city level, not precise location)
privacy.analyticsItem4: Referral sources (how you found our website)
privacy.thirdPartyText: PixelSwift uses Google Analytics 4 for website usage analysis. Google may process this data according to their own privacy policy. We do not use any advertising services, and we do not sell or share your data with any other third parties.
privacy.rightsText: You can opt out of Google Analytics tracking at any time by using the Google Analytics Opt-out Browser Add-on, enabling 'Do Not Track' in your browser settings, or using a content blocker. Opting out will not affect the functionality of PixelSwift.
privacy.contactText: If you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us at:

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.acceptText: By using PixelSwift, you agree to these terms of use. If you do not agree with these terms, please stop using the service.
terms.serviceText: PixelSwift provides free online image processing tools, including format conversion, image compression, and resizing. All processing is performed locally in your browser without requiring registration or login.
terms.usageItem1: You may use PixelSwift for personal or commercial purposes
terms.usageItem2: Do not use this tool to process illegal content
terms.usageItem3: Do not attempt to interfere with or disrupt the normal operation of this service
terms.ipText: The PixelSwift brand name, logo, and interface design are protected by intellectual property rights. Images you process through this tool remain yours, and we claim no rights over them.
terms.disclaimerText: PixelSwift is provided "as is" without any express or implied warranties. While we strive to ensure the reliability and accuracy of our tools, we are not responsible for any data loss or damage resulting from the use of this tool. We recommend keeping original backups before processing important images.
terms.changesText: We reserve the right to modify these terms of use at any time. Any changes will take effect immediately upon being posted on this page. Continued use of PixelSwift constitutes acceptance of the modified terms.
terms.contactText: If you have any questions about these terms of use, please contact us at:

contact.desc: If you have any questions, suggestions, or feedback, feel free to reach out to us.
